stemHtml: CS 25.125 specifies the requirements for determining the landing distance of an aeroplane. According to that rule, what is the earliest point at which wheel brakes may be applied by the pilot?

options.a: All wheels must be firmly on the ground before commencing any braking.
options.b: When only the main wheels are firmly on the ground, provided the nose gear can be lowered in a controlled manner.
options.c: Prior to touchdown because automatic braking systems must be used to determine the landing distance.
options.d: On first wheel touchdown, maximum manual braking is applied to achieve the shortest possible landing distance.
